title: A Plan The Muses Entertained.
content:
A plan the Muses entertain'd

    Methodically to impart

    To Psyche the poetic art;
    Prosaic-pure her soul remain'd.
    No wondrous sounds escaped her lyre

    E'en in the fairest Summer night;
    But Amor came with glance of fire, 

    The lesson soon was learn'd aright.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Johann Wolfgang von Goethe represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Johann Wolfgang von Goethe's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
